policy.statement: QCD simulation is a solver the theory earns the right to invoke once its parameters come out of the source side. The empirical published-compilation payload is the correction engine of the fine-structure lane; no simulated hadronic number enters any surface.
invocation_gate: source-side emission of the quark spread pair (blocked by the (R>0)^2 non-identifiability obstruction; runs/flavor/quark_spread_fiber_structure_transport_obstruction.json)
source-side bare-parameter rule with no experimental hadron target (schema requires target_calibration_present false)

solver.name: openQCD

build.toolchain: gcc-16 via open-mpi mpicc wrapper, portable flags (x86 intrinsics defines removed)
patches: modules/update/chrono.c: local typedef stack_t renamed oqcd_stack_t (macOS SDK collision)
modules/utils/error.c: static helper wait renamed oqcd_wait (POSIX collision)

solver.smoke_status: pure-gauge launch blocked by the sandboxed MPI runtime (PRRTE init); no trajectory was generated and no simulated number exists
solver.reflection_positivity_note: the unimproved Wilson action (csw = 0, plaquette gauge) is the declared target formulation because reflection positivity is a theorem there (Osterwalder-Seiler), discharging contract premise P4 by citation once invocation is earned

claim_boundary: This receipt records solver availability and the invocation gate. It produces no hadronic number, populates no production premise, and leaves every backend status field unchanged.
